data.title: EU Pushes for Stronger, Centralized Crypto Oversight
data.description:
The past week has marked another decisive shift in the European Union’s approach to crypto regulation. Even though the core package of the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework became fully applicable on December 30, 2024, the regulatory landscape continues to evolve.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/eu-pushes-for-stronger-centralized-crypto-oversight/

data.title: Byreal introduces social referral feature in Real Farmer
data.description:
In this post: Bybit’s social DeFi platform Byreal has expanded its flagship Real Farmer with a new referral feature that makes copyfarming more interactive and rewarding. Real Farmer allows users to pick LPs (liquidity providers) from a leaderboard ranking real-time liquidity positions and instantly copy their strategies. Rather than a full revamp, Bybit said its […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/byreal-introduces-social-referral-feature-in-real-farmer/

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences Significant Liquidations
data.description: According to TechFlow, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market experienced substantial liquidations amounting to $1.362 billion over the past 24 hours. Of this total, long positions accounted for $1.205 billion, while short positions made up $157 million. Globally, 278,102 individuals faced liquidations, with the largest single liquidation occurring on a centralized exchange (CEX) in the BTC-USDT pair, valued at $44.292 million.

data.title: SOL could dip below $120 as ETF inflows and sentiment weaken
data.description:
Key takeaways Solana is down 10% in the last 24 hours and is now trading below $140. The coin could dip further as the market sentiment weakens. Market sentiment weakens as cryptos suffer huge losses
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/sol-could-dip-below-120-as-etf-inflows-and-sentiment-weaken/

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description:
According to PANews, recent monitoring by Lookonchain reveals that ten Bitcoin ETFs have collectively seen a net outflow of 4,828 BTC, valued at approximately $460 million. Among these, BlackRock reported an outflow of 2,610 BTC, reducing its holdings to 794,948 BTC. Additionally, nine Ethereum ETFs experienced a net outflow of 70,248 ETH, worth around $221 million, with BlackRock accounting for 43,240 ETH of this outflow, bringing its holdings down to 3,828,719 ETH.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/32379303047850?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS
